executionArgs.additionalInstruction:
DEPLOYED APP URL (primary): https://control-app-six.vercel.app/ — Prefer navigating this production URL for all Playwright/browser tests. App uses HashRouter: use paths like https://control-app-six.vercel.app/#/, #/goals, #/journal, #/failures, #/trash, #/session, #/login. If runner only supports localhost, use http://127.0.0.1:5173/ with same hash paths.

AUTH BYPASS FOR LOCALHOST: Before testing against localhost, start the dev server from productivity-app/frontend with npm run dev:testsprite (loads committed .env.testsprite; VITE_AUTH_ENABLED=false). Plain npm run dev uses your local .env and may show Firebase login and rate limits.

VERCEL PRODUCTION: For a deploy without login, the Vercel project must set VITE_AUTH_ENABLED=false at build time. If login appears on production, check Environment Variables and redeploy.

CRITICAL: Do NOT stop testing if login fails or API returns errors. Skip auth: navigate directly to hash routes and explore protected UI shells; document any redirect to login.

Thoroughly verify: (1) Initial load and UI layout analysis. (2) All buttons and interactive elements clickable; detect blocked clicks (z-index overlays, pointer-events). (3) Navigate all pages: Dashboard, Goals, Journal, Failures, Trash (and Session). (4) Sidebar on desktop: nav links, Export dropdown open/close, Logout button layout and click, System Wipe modal cancel. (5) Mobile: bottom nav and responsiveness. (6) Modals, dropdowns, forms: open, close, submit where possible without valid API. (7) Console errors and failed network requests — log them. (8) Visual: overflow, alignment, spacing inconsistencies. (9) Performance: slow interactions, unnecessary re-renders if detectable.

Continue through the full suite even when steps fail; capture screenshots and evidence for each issue.
